figures.caption: FIG. 4. The orbital-projected DOSs of Ru-4d orbitals for different strained states. Spin-up and Spin-down DOSs are represented by the positive and negative values, respectively. (a): The NM

FIG. 2. The physical properties of RuO2 calculated with different values of U eff . (a) The relative total energies of the NM, AM 1, and AM 2 states. The black vertical lines divide the U eff parameter space into three different magnetic phases. (b) The Ru spin magnetic moments of the NM, AM 1, and AM 2 states. (c) The Fermi level’s DOS (black squares) of the NM state and the corresponding ρ(EF) · U eff values (red spheres).

FIG. 5. The electronic band structures of strained RuO2 with (a): uniaxial strain along c axis, (b): isotropic biaxial tensile strain along a and b axes, and (c): equal amounts of tensile strain and compressive strain along a and b axes, respectively. The up spin and down spin bands are represented by the black and red lines, respectively. The featured spin-splitting in the middle of M-Γ and A-Z between flat bands are marked by the blue, green or yellow boxes.

FIG. 3. The NM-magnetic phase diagram of strained RuO2 bulk. The magnitude of spin magnetic moment M Ru for different strained states are represented by the chroma. a, b, and c axes of RuO2 are adjusted in the range 94% - 106% of their experimental values (a = b = 4.49 Å, c = 3.11 Å) [5], with the step of 2% to sample the whole strained-state space. The intersection points of the grid lines mark the actually calculated points. The intermediate areas between calculated points are completed by linear interpolation.

FIG. 1. (a) The atomic structure of rutile RuO2. The local Cartesian coordinate system is set on the central Ru atom in the unit cell. r 1 and r 2 are the Ru-O bond lengths in the x-y plane and along z direction, respectively. α and β are the O-Ru-O bond angles in the x-y plane. The Ru and O atoms used for orbital projection are also marked by the black lines. (b) The orbital-projected electronic DOS of RuO2 based on the local coordinate system introduced in (a). (c) The spin occupation on the 4d orbitals of the NM state suggested by DFT calculations. (d) The collinear AM structure of RuO2. The two Ru sublattices are represented by RuA and RuB , respectively. The blue and red arrows represent different spin magnetic moments for each Ru sublattice. (e)
